name: Quartz Glass Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Quartz Glass. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Quartz Glass cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength with good transmission through Quartz Glass for selective contaminant removal

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning with adequate energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with controlled thermal accumulation

fluenceThreshold.description: Energy density threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Quartz Glass substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with minimal substrate heating

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning systems (e.g., CleanLaser CL 50-500 models) on delicate substrates like quartz glass to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for controlled ablation of contaminants. Max safe ~300-500W depending on fluence to prevent cracking (source: IPG Photonics specs and J. Laser Appl. 2018 study on glass cleaning).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for 1064 nm cleaning; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (1000-2000 mm/s) for light; 500 mm/s recommended for optimal balance on quartz to minimize heat input (source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals and Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020 on scan optimization).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for cost-effective cleaning of quartz glass; 5-50 ns for ns lasers, ps (10-100 ps) for minimal HAZ but higher cost; 20 ns recommended to reduce thermal effects given quartz's low absorption at 1064 nm (source: Laser physics in Appl. Phys. A 2019 on pulse regimes for dielectrics).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning; 20-50 kHz for controlled energy delivery, up to 100 kHz for faster processing but risk of heat accumulation on low-conductivity quartz; 50 kHz balances speed and safety (source: Academic lit. in J. Appl. Phys. 2021 on rep rate trade-offs for transparent substrates).

energy_density.description: Fluence for contaminant ablation without substrate damage; damage threshold for quartz ~10-15 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses; 1.5 J/cm² recommended for safe cleaning of organics/metals on glass (source: Ablation studies in Opt. Express 2017 on fused silica thresholds).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precision vs. area coverage; 50-200 μm for fine detail on quartz surfaces, 200-500 μm for larger areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning without hotspots (source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on quartz while staying below damage threshold (source: Industry case studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. 2022 on multi-pass laser cleaning of glass).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 30-50% for speed, 50-80% for thorough cleaning; 50% recommended for quartz to avoid uneven heating given its thermal properties (source: Process optimization in Lasers Manuf. Mater. Process. 2019).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.citation: H. K. Sun, T. F. Bencala, S. J. J. Kwok, and M. D. Feit, Applied Optics, Vol. 34, Issue 21, pp. 4690-4694, 1995, DOI: 10.1364/AO.34.004690
laserDamageThreshold.context: High-purity fused silica (equivalent to quartz glass, >99.99% SiO2), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm wavelength, 1 ns pulse duration, single-shot (1-on-1) damage testing

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds
